title: The Romaunt of the Rose
summary: CME00038 is the General Corpus XML representation of the Middle English Romaunt of the Rose alone. The University of Michigan encoding derives from Max Kaluza's 1891 bilingual parallel edition but excludes its interleaved Old French column. Kaluza transcribed the extant English text principally from University of Glasgow Library MS Hunter 409 (V.3.7), supplied the manuscript's lost passages from William Thynne's 1532 print, and recorded corrections or variants from earlier editions. This graph distinguishes the intellectual work from its manuscript witnesses, printed editions, electronic encoding, repository artifact, and digital facsimiles. The complete 303-image folio sequence publicly exposed for Hunter 409 and a complete scan of Kaluza's volume are cached; the limits and provider terms of each surrogate are explicit.
review_notes: The cataloging subject is the extant Middle English translation/adaptation, not the French Roman de la Rose, Hunter 409 as a physical object, Kaluza's bilingual volume, or the U-M encoding.

Fragment A (lines 1–1705), Fragment B (1706–5810), and Fragment C (5811–7696) are durable scholarly attribution units, not divisions visibly imposed by Hunter 409; the manuscript presents a continuous poem.

The phrase 'unique Glasgow manuscript' is inherited from Kaluza and the Glasgow project. Hunter 409 is the sole near-complete multi-folio witness, but a detached Romaunt leaf in NLS Acc.10225 is a second fragmentary witness.

A cached manuscript bundle marked complete is complete for Glasgow's published sequence of fols. 1r–151v and 152r. The provider exposes no covers, boards, spine, pastedowns, edges, or f. 152v and no larger public profile was found.
Textual lineation, physical foliation, printed pagination, PDF capture pages, and provider sequence positions are recorded as separate locator systems.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00038
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by the repository publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00038.
The XML contains the two edition title pages and the Middle English poem, but not Kaluza's parallel French column.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00038
entities.description: Public electronic text titled The Romaunt of the Rose [ME text only], encoding Kaluza's title pages and Middle English text while omitting the interleaved French original.

entities.label: Max Kaluza, The Romaunt of the Rose, Part I—The Texts (1891)
alternate_labels: The Romaunt of the Rose: From the Unique Glasgow MS, Parallel with its Original Le Roman de la Rose
entities.description: Bilingual parallel edition with the Middle English text on odd-numbered pages and the Old French source on facing even-numbered pages. Kaluza used Hunter 409 for the extant English text, Thynne's 1532 print for lost and locally deficient passages, and earlier editions for cited corrections and variants.

bibliographic.citation: The Romaunt of the Rose: From the Unique Glasgow MS, Parallel with its Original Le Roman de la Rose, edited by Max Kaluza, Part I—The Texts, Chaucer Society Publications, First Series 83 (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co. for the Chaucer Society, 1891).
bibliographic.title: The Romaunt of the Rose: From the Unique Glasgow MS, Parallel with its Original Le Roman de la Rose
bibliographic.publisher: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co. for the Chaucer Society

notes: In the cached PDF, the main title page is PDF p. 10, the part title is p. 14, English printed pp. 3–391 are on PDF pp. 16, 18, …, 404, and English printed pp. 393–439 are on PDF pp. 408, 410, …, 454. Two publisher-circular pages intervene at PDF pp. 406–407.
U-M encodes the discontiguous English odd pages and excludes the facing French even pages.

entities.label: University of Glasgow Library MS Hunter 409
entities.description: The sole near-complete multi-folio manuscript witness of the Middle English Romaunt, copied in England by one principal scribe with corrections or additions by a second hand and later marginalia. The poem now begins imperfectly at line 45 on f. 1r and ends at line 7692 on f. 150r; eleven leaves are reported lost.

date_statements.label: Provider manuscript dating
date_statements.value: first quarter of the fifteenth century; other Glasgow pages describe c. 1440–1450
date_statements.notes: The conflicting institutional descriptions are preserved rather than silently reconciled.

holding.notes: William Hunter collection; rebound by S. M. Cockerell and Son in 1963.

physical_description.support: Support not verified from the consulted public institutional descriptions
physical_description.extent: The public folio sequence exposes fols. 1r–151v and 152r; the Romaunt occupies current fols. 1r–150r
notes: Hybrid anglicana formata script in black ink; one principal scribe, with second-hand corrections or additions and later marginalia.
Eleven lost leaves are stated in Kaluza's headnote; Glasgow's summary bullets describe the losses inconsistently and require reconciliation.
Lines 7109–7158 and 7159 are displaced relative to lines 7013–7302, an order also reflected in Thynne's print.

entities.label: William Thynne, The workes of Geffray Chaucer (1532)
entities.description: The first collected Chaucer print edited by William Thynne. Its Romaunt text is manuscript-dependent and is Kaluza's source for passages absent or locally deficient in Hunter 409.

bibliographic.citation: Geoffrey Chaucer, The workes of Geffray Chaucer newly printed, with dyuers workes whiche were neuer in print before, edited by William Thynne (London: Thomas Godfray, 1532), STC 5068.
bibliographic.title: The workes of Geffray Chaucer newly printed, with dyuers workes whiche were neuer in print before

notes: University of Glasgow Library's comparison project uses its copy, Sp Coll Hunterian Bs.2.17; the completeness of publicly exposed images relative to that entire printed volume was not established.

entities.label: Richard Morris, The Poetical Works of Geoffrey Chaucer, volume VI ([1872])
entities.description: The new and revised Bell and Daldy edition identified by Kaluza's siglum M as a source of corrections or variants.

bibliographic.citation: Geoffrey Chaucer, The Poetical Works of Geoffrey Chaucer, with Poems Formerly Printed with His or Attributed to Him, edited by Richard Morris, new and revised ed., vol. 6 (London: Bell and Daldy, [1872]).
bibliographic.title: The Poetical Works of Geoffrey Chaucer

entities.label: Robert Bell, The Poetical Works of Geoffrey Chaucer (1854–1856)
entities.description: Eight-volume edition associated with Kaluza's siglum B. Kaluza's headnote names Bell but does not give a full citation, so the identification remains supported rather than certain at volume and issue level.

bibliographic.citation: Geoffrey Chaucer, The Poetical Works of Geoffrey Chaucer, edited by Robert Bell, with a memoir by John M. Jephson, 8 vols. (London: John W. Parker and Son, 1854–1856).
bibliographic.title: The Poetical Works of Geoffrey Chaucer

entities.label: John Urry, The Works of Geoffrey Chaucer (1721)
entities.description: Posthumous Chaucer edition identified by Kaluza's siglum U as a source of corrections or variants.

bibliographic.citation: Geoffrey Chaucer, The Works of Geoffrey Chaucer, edited by John Urry (London: Bernard Lintot, 1721).
bibliographic.title: The Works of Geoffrey Chaucer

entities.label: Francisque Michel, Le Roman de la Rose (1864)
entities.description: Two-volume French edition furnishing most of Kaluza's facing Old French text, which he additionally collated with French manuscripts. This component is not present in CME00038.

bibliographic.citation: Guillaume de Lorris and Jean de Meun, Le Roman de la Rose, edited by Francisque Michel, nouvelle édition revue et corrigée, 2 vols. (Paris: Firmin Didot frères, fils et Cie, 1864).
bibliographic.title: Le Roman de la Rose
bibliographic.publisher: Firmin Didot frères, fils et Cie

entities.label: Detached Romaunt of the Rose leaf in NLS Acc.10225
entities.description: A detached leaf reported among the papers of the Reverend James Maxwell Joass in the Sutherland Estate Papers, transmitting Romaunt lines 2403–2450. It is collateral evidence for the work, not an ancestor of the 1891 Kaluza edition.

holding.notes: The linked resource is a related Sutherland Estates catalogue record that refers to the main series as Acc.10225; it is not an exact component-level record for the leaf.

physical_description.support: Support not verified from a public item-level description
physical_description.extent: One detached leaf, two sides; Romaunt lines 2403–2450
notes: Lines 2403–2442 overlap the passage absent from current Hunter 409; lines 2443–2450 overlap text still present there.
No public digital images or exact item-level catalogue record were found in the dated search.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Kaluza's 1891 Romaunt of the Rose
entities.description: A 461-page Google scan from the Harvard University copy, preserved by Internet Archive with item metadata and scandata. The complete volume, including both English and French columns and publisher matter, is retained.
entities.reproduction_format: 461-page access PDF with Internet Archive metadata and scandata

entities.label: University of Glasgow published folio sequence for MS Hunter 409
entities.description: The full sequence publicly indexed by Glasgow's Romaunt project: 303 500-pixel-wide JPEG page images, fols. 1r–151v plus 152r. It includes the work at fols. 1r–150r and exposed post-text flyleaves, but no covers, boards, spine, pastedowns, edges, or f. 152v.
entities.reproduction_format: 303 provider JPEG resources bundled from a mechanically generated URL inventory

agents.name: Max Kaluza
notes: Editor, transcriber, and collator of the 1891 bilingual edition; not the medieval translator.

agents.name: University of Glasgow Library, Archives & Special Collections
William Thynne
notes: Editor of the 1532 Chaucer collection; not the medieval translator of the Romaunt.

scope.notes: Repository history identifies the U-M Corpus of Middle English as the upstream electronic-text source.

subject.description: Title pages and Middle English text encoded by U-M

object.description: Title pages and English odd-page column only
locators: main title and part title; printed English pp. 3–439

scope.notes: The facing Old French text and publisher circular are excluded.

subject.description: Middle English column where Hunter 409 survives
locators: printed English pp. 3–439, excluding supplied lacunae and local supplements
current fols. 1r–150r; manuscript lines 45–7692 with losses

scope.notes: Kaluza says the English closely follows V.3.7; the relation does not cover the portions he explicitly supplied from Thynne.

object.description: Corresponding Romaunt readings in the 1532 print

scope.notes: The eleven full-leaf losses and the smaller beginning/end defects are distinguished.

subject.description: Romaunt text demonstrably dependent on Hunter 409

object.description: Corresponding passages in the manuscript's sixteenth-century state

scope.notes: Printer marks in Hunter 409 and the shared displaced-leaf order establish use of this manuscript, but do not by themselves prove that every 1532 Romaunt reading derives solely from it.

assertion.notes: The siglum and surname are explicit, but the precise volume or issue was not verified from Kaluza's abbreviated statement.

subject.description: Facing Old French column excluded from CME00038

object.description: Michel's French text used as Kaluza's principal copy text

scope.notes: Kaluza also collated other French manuscripts; this relation is outside the encoded Middle English selection.

subject.description: All 303 images in Glasgow's published folio sequence

object.description: All manuscript surfaces exposed by the provider

scope.notes: Completeness is relative to the provider inventory, not the entire physical binding.

access.access_method: Read the tracked XML from the initialized CME submodule at the pinned commit.
Open the U-M item or full-text view in a browser.
Download the complete PDF and its item metadata/scandata from Internet Archive.
access.format: PDF plus Internet Archive XML metadata

work_portion.label: Middle English Romaunt text encoded in CME00038
locators: printed English pp. 3–439 on odd-numbered pages
notes: The cached object is the complete volume; U-M selected the English column and omitted the interleaved French even pages.
Main title and part title occur at PDF pp. 10 and 14.

access.access_method: Use the project's folio index to open each provider JPEG; the local bundle preserves every indexed image without upscaling.

local_copies.label: Complete Glasgow-published folio inventory for Hunter 409

work_portion.label: Extant manuscript text of The Romaunt of the Rose in Hunter 409
notes: The text starts imperfectly with line 45 and ends with manuscript line 7692.
The bundle retains the provider's post-text images f. 150v–152r because the requested object boundary is the entire published manuscript sequence.
The eleven leaves now lost cannot appear; Kaluza supplies their text from Thynne's 1532 print.
Complete means all 303 images exposed by the provider index: fols. 1r–151v and 152r.
No covers, boards, spine, pastedowns, edges, or f. 152v are exposed in that index.
Every image is the provider's 500-pixel-wide resource; no IIIF service or 1600–1800-pixel public profile was found, and no image was upscaled.

The cache is retained for private, personal and non-commercial research under Glasgow's provider terms; redistribution, publication, modification, separated reuse, or commercial use requires separate review or permission.

access.access_method: Consult through Archives & Special Collections' reading room or virtual reading-room enquiry process; arrangements and handling restrictions apply.
Request new or publication-quality reproductions and permission through Archives & Special Collections.
Use Glasgow's Romaunt comparison pages, which place selected pages from Sp Coll Hunterian Bs.2.17 beside Hunter 409.
access.format: HTML comparison pages and JPEG images
notes: The project says the manuscript and Glasgow's Thynne copy were digitized in their entirety, but no authoritative public inventory of the complete physical printed volume was verified; no complete-volume cache claim is made.

access.access_method: Dated searches of the NLS manuscript catalogue and public web surfaces found no item-level digital images.
notes: The linked resource is related to the Sutherland Estates papers and references Acc.10225; it is not a digital surrogate or an exact leaf record.

access.access_method: Ask NLS to identify the exact Acc.10225 component and quote for full two-sided imaging including edges and support details; condition, copyright, and permission checks apply.

rights.component: U-M electronic text and displayed Middle English content
rights.basis: The U-M item labels the materials public domain and links the DPLA No Copyright–United States rights statement.

rights.component: 1891 editorial text, typography, and underlying medieval texts
rights.basis: The 1891 publication is beyond the ordinary U.S. copyright term; U-M marks its source materials public domain and Internet Archive metadata reports NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT for the U.S.
notes: This copyright conclusion is separate from conditions attached to a particular provider's scan.

rights.jurisdiction: United States; provider access terms may have broader application
rights.basis: Internet Archive metadata reports NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT and identifies the U.S. copyright region. The Google front notice separately requests personal, non-commercial use and attribution and warns that public-domain status can vary by country.
notes: Copyright status and provider-requested use conditions are not conflated.

rights.component: University of Glasgow manuscript photographs and project presentation
rights.basis: No image-specific open license was found. Glasgow's terms permit viewing, downloading, and printing for personal and non-commercial use, but require prior written consent for other or commercial use, attribution, and restrictions on modification or separated reuse of illustrations and photographs.
notes: Private research caching is supported by the stated personal/non-commercial permission; redistribution, publication, modification, and commercial use require a fresh rights review or permission.

rights.component: 1532 printed text and underlying medieval work
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1532 and is beyond the ordinary U.S. copyright term.

rights.component: Any reproduction supplied by NLS; no reproduction obtained
rights.basis: NLS states that copying and reuse depend on condition, copyright, intended use, and any third-party rights; no item-specific image or license was available.

editorial_practices.representation: Title pages and Middle English text only
editorial_practices.description: U-M selected the Middle English column from Kaluza's bilingual volume and omitted the interleaved Old French original.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Hunter 409's extant text and physical lacunae
editorial_practices.representation: Manuscript text supplemented from Thynne's 1532 print
editorial_practices.description: Kaluza follows Hunter 409 where it survives, labels full missing-leaf ranges and smaller defective line beginnings or endings, and supplies those portions from Thynne.

editorial_practices.representation: Footnote apparatus citing M, B, U, and Kaluza's own emendations
editorial_practices.description: Kaluza records corrections or variants from Morris, Bell, and Urry and distinguishes his own editorial emendations; the French column principally follows Michel but was also collated with other French manuscripts.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00038.xml
evidence.locator: IDG; BODY/DIV1[1–3]; title pages, headnote, poem, notes, page breaks, milestones, trailer
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for repository identity, the inherited edition title pages, the encoded Middle English selection, textual structure, lineation, editorial notes, and explicit ending.

evidence.citation: Local structural and checksum inspection of CME00038.xml
evidence.summary: Confirmed three top-level DIV1 units, 7,712 L elements of which 7,696 belong to the main poem stream and 16 occur inside notes, 199 LG elements of which 196 are in the main stream, 221 PB, 2,546 MILESTONE, 1,475 NOTE, and 20 P elements. It also confirmed the exact file hash and legacy identifiers.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English, CME00038 item record
evidence.summary: Identifies the item as The Romaunt of the Rose [ME text only], cites the 1891 Chaucer Society edition, exposes the title-page/title-page-of-part/poem contents, and supplies a stable U-M identifier.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English, CME00038 full poem view
evidence.summary: Gives Kaluza's statement that the English closely follows Glasgow V.3.7, enumerates the eleven full-leaf passages and smaller defects supplied from Thynne, and defines the M, B, and U correction sigla.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan rights statement for CME00038
evidence.summary: Labels the source materials public domain and associates the item with the DPLA No Copyright–United States statement.

evidence.citation: Max Kaluza, ed., The Romaunt of the Rose, Part I—The Texts (1891), complete Harvard/Google scan
evidence.summary: Direct evidence for the edition's full title, publication, bilingual facing-page structure, English text and apparatus, French copy-text statement, physical pagination, terminal text, and Google public-domain notice.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for romauntrosefrom00chaugoog
evidence.summary: Identifies the 1891 edition, Harvard source copy, Google digitization, 2006 scan date, OCLC number, and NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT status in the U.S.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive scandata for romauntrosefrom00chaugoog
evidence.summary: Supplies the ordered 461-page scan structure used to reconcile PDF capture pages and printed pagination.

evidence.citation: University of Glasgow, The Romaunt of the Rose Project: Background and Pilot Project
evidence.summary: Institutional project account of Hunter 409, its script, date, lacunae, Fragment A/B/C line ranges and attribution debate, Thynne's use, displaced leaves, NLS detached leaf, and the project's digitization.
notes: The page's prose says eleven leaves are lost, but its itemized bullet quantities do not sum consistently to eleven.

evidence.citation: University of Glasgow Romaunt project folio index
evidence.summary: Provider index exposing the ordered folio pages used to build the complete 303-image inventory, from f. 1r through f. 151v and then f. 152r.

evidence.citation: Direct inspection and mechanical inventory of University of Glasgow Hunter 409 page images
evidence.summary: Confirmed 303 unique provider images, the work boundary at fols. 1r–150r, retained post-text folios, 500-pixel resource width, and absence from the public index of covers, spine, edges, pastedowns, and f. 152v.

evidence.citation: University of Glasgow Special Collections, Literary Manuscripts
evidence.summary: Describes Hunter 409 as a unique manuscript of the Romaunt, dates it c. 1440–1450, identifies evidence of Thynne's use, and notes the project comparison with the 1532 print.

evidence.citation: University of Glasgow Special Collections, Book of the Month: The Romaunt of the Rose
evidence.summary: Institutional description of the manuscript's date, content, scribal character, Hunter history, and 1963 rebinding.

evidence.citation: University of Glasgow website terms of use
evidence.summary: Permits viewing, downloading, and printing for personal and non-commercial use and internal non-commercial circulation, while requiring prior consent for other or commercial use, acknowledgement, and compliance with restrictions on modification and separated use of photographs or illustrations.

evidence.citation: University of Glasgow Archives & Special Collections enquiries
evidence.summary: Provides reading-room and virtual-reading-room routes, contact information, quick-snap limits, and the route for requesting reproductions or publication support.

evidence.citation: University of Glasgow permission to reproduce and fee schedule
evidence.summary: States that formal permission and fees may be required for reproduction and publication unless an explicit exception or open license applies.

evidence.citation: Folger Shakespeare Library catalog record 161594, Chaucer, The workes (1532)
evidence.summary: Confirms the 1532 title, William Thynne's editorial responsibility, Thomas Godfray imprint, and STC 5068 identity.

evidence.citation: Folger Shakespeare Library catalog record 79291, Chaucer's Poetical Works edited by Richard Morris
evidence.summary: Supports the bibliographic identity of Morris's new and revised Bell and Daldy edition and its Romaunt-bearing volume.

evidence.citation: University of Pennsylvania Online Books Page, Robert Bell bibliography
evidence.summary: Supports identification of Robert Bell's 1854–1856 eight-volume Poetical Works as the edition most plausibly represented by Kaluza's B siglum.

evidence.citation: Folger Shakespeare Library catalog record 795012, Works of Geoffrey Chaucer edited by John Urry (1721)
evidence.summary: Confirms the title, John Urry's editorial responsibility, 1721 London publication, and Bernard Lintot imprint.

evidence.citation: HathiTrust catalog record 006708279, Le Roman de la Rose, edited by Francisque Michel (1864)
evidence.summary: Confirms the bibliographic identity of Michel's two-volume 1864 French edition, named by Kaluza as his principal source for the facing French text.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland manuscripts catalogue, related Sutherland Estates resource
evidence.summary: A related institutional catalogue record identifies the main Sutherland Estates series as Acc.10225, but does not expose an exact component-level description or image of the Romaunt leaf.
notes: This is negative and routing evidence, not an exact catalog identification of the leaf.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland copying services
evidence.summary: Describes requestable copying, fees, condition and copyright checks, and the distinction between research scans and publication-quality reproduction.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland copyright guidance
evidence.summary: Explains that reproduction and reuse may require item-specific permissions and consideration of third-party rights.

open_questions.question: How should Hunter 409's date be reconciled between Glasgow's 'first quarter of the fifteenth century' and c. 1440–1450 descriptions?
next_steps: Consult the current University of Glasgow manuscript catalogue or obtain an Archives & Special Collections response.
Prefer current codicological scholarship over inherited exhibition prose if the evidence differs.

open_questions.question: How should the traditional 7,696-line work extent be reconciled with Hunter 409's terminal line 7692, the XML's 7,696 main-stream L elements, and Kaluza's terminal note or alternative lines?
next_steps: Collate Kaluza's final printed pages, XML milestones and nested notes, and Hunter fol. 150r line by line.
Record distinct manuscript, edition, and encoding line-number systems rather than forcing one total.

open_questions.question: What is the exact Acc.10225 component number, date, physical description, and imaging status of the NLS detached Romaunt leaf?
next_steps: Ask NLS to identify the component within Acc.10225 and provide current catalog metadata.
Request full recto and verso images including all edges and a scale, subject to rights and handling review.
Compare lines 2403–2450 directly with Hunter 409 and Thynne only after obtaining the surrogate.

open_questions.question: Why does Glasgow's itemized lost-leaf summary fail to total the eleven leaves stated in its prose and Kaluza's exact supplied line ranges?
next_steps: Reconstruct the quire losses from a current codicological description.
Confirm whether the losses after current fols. 71 and 148 comprise two and four leaves respectively, as implied by Kaluza's line ranges.

open_questions.question: Can Glasgow provide a current full-object surrogate of Hunter 409 including binding, covers, boards, spine, pastedowns, edges, and f. 152v, and what reuse permission would govern it?
next_steps: Ask Archives & Special Collections whether additional master images exist and request an object-complete reproduction quote.
Obtain explicit publication and redistribution terms before any use beyond the private cache.

open_questions.question: What are the exact creation date, keying workflow, quality-control history, and source-copy provenance of U-M encoding CME00038?
next_steps: Consult U-M collection-level technical documentation or ask the library for item-level production metadata.
